notes: Layer 2 now reflects the mounted POST route instead of claiming the feature has no behavioral surface. The two API checks are pending until a branch runtime can be called safely. Layer 1 evidence at the exact pre-commit worktree state: focused Rybbit service + mounted-route tests passed 27/27 across 2 files; the full backend suite passed 1,246/1,246 across 68 files; TypeScript and strict conventions exited 0 with 0 clear structural violations. That evidence is not substituted for running-app Layer 2 proof. Renderer injection, the no-PII/cookieless audit, the privacy-policy decision, and a live preview observation remain mandatory activation preconditions; none is passed or waived here.

items.title: Disabled preview provisioning returns the canonical route response
items.precondition: Run this branch in a safe environment with PREVIEW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED absent or not equal to the exact string true, and authenticate as a super-admin.
steps: POST /api/admin/websites/{projectId}/integrations/rybbit/preview with the super-admin bearer token.
Capture the HTTP status and JSON body.
Confirm the selected project and its Rybbit integration state are unchanged.
items.expected: HTTP 200 with { success:true, data:{ enabled:false, provisioned:false, reason:"gate_disabled" } }; no provider site is created and no local Rybbit site id/integration is written.
items.notes: Layer 1 route coverage passes through the real Express router and middleware stack, but this item stays pending until the callable branch runtime is exercised.

items.title: Preview provisioning route enforces authentication and super-admin access
items.precondition: Run this branch in a safe environment with one valid non-super-admin token available.
steps: Repeat with a valid non-super-admin bearer token.
Confirm neither rejected request changes project or integration state.
items.expected: The unauthenticated request returns HTTP 401; the authenticated non-super-admin request returns HTTP 403; neither reaches provisioning.
items.notes: The focused Supertest suite proves the middleware order in-process. This remains pending for Layer 2 because no running branch endpoint was called.
